(+)-trans-Khellactone can be synthesized via epoxidation of xanthyletin using iminium salt 710 and TPPP, followed by acid-promoted epoxide ring opening, selective oxidation, and esterification with 3,3-dimethyl acryloyl chloride.
Triptolide is a potent diterpenoid triepoxide whose medicinal properties can be optimized through synthetic organic strategies.
(-)-triptolide is a natural product that can be accessed via a scalable, asymmetric synthetic route involving 9 or 10 steps, resulting in a total yield of 13.6% or 18.5%, respectively.
Cinnamoylquinic acid, a structural analogue of caffeoylquinic acid, undergoes acyl migration under basic and heating (90°C) conditions, with no detectable intermolecular migration.
Dibenzyl glutamate can be coupled with hydrolyzed C-6 acetyl glycosides to form new glycosidic derivatives.
The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be efficiently synthesized via base-catalyzed Diels-Alder reactions of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones, achieving good yields and exclusive endo selectivity.
6-epi-ampicillin can degrade under room temperature in pyridineacetic acid solution over five days, leading to the isolation and detection of a diketopiperazine.

The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be constructed via intramolecular Diels-Alder (DA) reactions of halogenated 2-pyrones, including methods using base- or acid-induced equilibration of 2H-pyran-2,5-diones.

2-O-benzyl-6-deoxy-L-arabino-hexos-5-ulose can be synthesized from lactose and serves as a precursor for the preparation of 1,6-di-deoxy-D-galacto and 1,6-di-deoxy-L-altro nojirimycin derivatives via reductive aminocyclization.
2'-deoxymethanocarba adenosine can be synthesized in both N-locked and S-locked versions for use in conformationally locked nucleoside analogues.
Saframycin-A is a potent anticancer alkaloid that can be synthesized using small aromatic analogues of phenanthroquinolizidines as intermediates.
6-amino-penicillanic acid benzyl ester is used as a coupling partner in the synthesis of both triprotected and [6alpha-3H]-labeled penicillin N derivatives.
The tricyclic core of basiliolide B can be constructed using a base-catalyzed aldol reaction and subsequent synthetic transformations.
Zinc DL-methionine is more effective than other zinc salts in attenuating biochemical consequences of oxygen free radicals in vitro.
Phenamide exhibits anti-Staphylococcus aureus activity among the tested compounds.
Cysteine-sulphite can prevent respiratory inhibition caused by increased oxalacetate accumulation in manometric runs.

N 5 -(1-Imino-2-chloroethyl)-L-ornithine is a suitable tool for studying methylarginine-mediated regulation of nitric oxide production and is a potential therapeutic lead for conditions with elevated nitric oxide levels.
S-allylmercaptoglutathione possesses SH-modifying properties and antioxidant activity.
L-glutamic acid-gamma-hydrazide, when injected into mice, decreases glutamate decarboxylase (GAD) activity and increases GABA levels in the brain.
Gamma-D-glutamylamino-methylsulfonate is a glutamate receptor antagonist that prevents toxicity in rat hippocampal slices under ischemic conditions.
L-ornithine hydrobromide supports growth and nicotinic acid production in Mycobacterium tuberculosis when used as a nitrogen source in the Proskauer and Beck synthetic medium.
L-ornithine hydrobromide supports growth and production of nicotinic acid in Mycobacterium tuberculosis hominis when used as a sole carbon source.
L-glutamic acid-gamma-hydrazide induces GABA accumulation in the substantia nigra of rats, and this effect is reduced on the side opposite to the posterior hypothalamic hemisection.
Arginine-ascorbate, administered orally or intravenously, is recommended as a treatment for moderate to severe COVID-19 infections.
Leucylvinylphosphonic acid derivative 1 exhibits antimicrobial activity comparable to dehydrophos.

2,9-diamino-5,6-dicarboxy-4,7-dithiadecanedioic acid is a chemically stable and selective antidote for Hg(II) poisoning with inherently low toxicity, forming reasonably stable complexes with several divalent metal ions.
Glycine phosphoramidate derivatives (3a-b) exhibit antiviral activity against HIV-1 and HIV-2 replication in vitro, with alanine derivatives being about 10 times more potent than glycine derivatives.
Intravenous arginine-ascorbate treatment can result in undetectable hepatitis C viral loads 5 years after therapy in patients who were not cleared after two treatments with ribavirin and interferon.

Arginine-ascorbate exhibits synergistic antiviral action by enhancing the antiviral response via interferons and inducing the expression of over 850 novel host cell genes, which is sufficient to completely block replication and spread of myxoma virus in human fibroblasts and to inhibit the spread of vaccinia virus and tanapox virus to neighboring cells.
Pyloricidin B, a peptidic moiety composed of L-valine-L-leucine, exhibits potent and highly selective anti-Helicobacter pylori activity.
Arsinothricin exhibits broad-spectrum antibiotic activity.

Salts of 2-amino-3-cyclopropylpropanoic acid, as present in compound 2, exhibit broad-spectrum antiviral activity against influenza viruses in vitro, with an IC50 of 4.73 µg/mL, CC50 > 300 µg/mL, and a selectivity index (SI) of 64.

Compounds containing residues of 2-amino-3-cyclopropylpropanoic acid salts exhibit antiviral activity against oseltamivir-resistant A/Vladivostok/2/09 (H1N1) and A/mallard/Pennsylvania/1984 (H5N2) avian influenza strains.
(E)-N-methoxy cinnamamide undergoes Pd-catalyzed C-H functionalization that selectively activates the alpha-C-H bond, yielding both E- and Z-isomers, with Z-isomer predominating.
4-hydroxychalcone exhibits anti-aromatase activity, and its inhibitory effect is reduced when hydroxylated at position 2Ј, resulting in 2Ј,4-dihydroxychalcone.
4-hydroxychalcone is the most potent inhibitor of 17␤-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase among the compounds studied.
4-hydroxychalcone, a chalconoid with one OH group eliminated, exhibits a loss in activity of approximately 20-fold compared to chalconoids with both OH groups present.
In the AmpC/4-(2-carboxyvinyl)phenyl boronic acid complex (1KE0), the carboxylate group of the inhibitor interacts with a single ordered water molecule in the AmpC binding site.

(Z)-3,4,5-Trihydroxycinnamic acid is a derivative of caffeic acid obtained from E-3,4,5-trimethoxycinnamic acid and is characterized by 1H NMR (CD3OD, 500 MHz) signals at δ 7.6 (1H, d, J = 9.1 Hz; H-3), 6.91 (1H, s; H-6), 6.72 (1H, s; H-2), 6.14 (1H, d, J = 9.1 Hz; H-2), and 13C NMR (CD3OD, 125 MHz) signals at δ 162.9, 150.7, 149.1, 144.7, 143.2, 111.7, 111.5, 111.1, 102.3.
Transcinnamate is produced from L-phenylalanine by the non-oxidative enzymatic reaction catalyzed by phenylalanine ammonia-lyase in higher plants.
trans-cinnamate is produced from L-phenylalanine by the enzyme phenylalanine ammonia-lyase (PAL) and serves as a precursor for various phenylpropanoids such as lignins, £avonoids, and coumarins.
trans-cinnamate is the reaction product monitored at 290 nm (O 290 = 10^4 M/31 cm^3) to determine PAL enzyme activity.
(E)-2-oxo-4-phenyl-3-butenoic acid acts as an inhibitor of pyruvate decarboxylase, and its complexes with the enzyme produce an enamine intermediate detectable by a 440 nm absorbance maximum.
In clostridium, (E)-cinnamate is formed by dehydration of (R)-phenyllactate and is subsequently reduced to phenylpropionate.

Trans-cinnamate serves as a substrate for NADPH-dependent hydroxylation in P450 enzymes, with activity in microsomes of transgenic yeast showing 3.5, 29, and 0.9% of wild type activity for Pro448Ile, Pro448Phe, and Pro448Val mutants, respectively.

Phenyl-substituted E-4-phenyl-2-keto-3-butenoic acid derivatives can act as time-dependent irreversible inhibitors of brewer's yeast pyruvate decarboxylase (EC 4.1.1.1), with electron-withdrawing substituents conferring this property.

(E)-2-oxo-4-phenyl-3-butenoic acid type inhibitors/alternate substrates enable detection of an enzyme-bound enamine intermediate at 440 nm in both pdc1 and pdc1-6 fusion enzymes of brewers' yeast pyruvate decarboxylase.
SHAM is a known inhibitor of lipoxygenase (LOX) and is used at 10 mM concentration in plant tissue treatment.
SHAM treatment abolishes the expression of ACO and SAMDC genes and slightly weakens PR-2 expression in tomato leaves after 3 days of T. urticae infestation.
SHAM inhibits intracellular ROS accumulation and stomatal closure in tobacco cells.
SHA, an aromatic acid inhibitor of APX peroxidase activity, forms a hydrogen bond between its iron-coordinating oxygen and the NH of Trp-41 in the peroxidase active site.
Acrylic salicylic acid is used as an antiplatelet agent in combination with clopidogrel following LMCA stent implantation.
Salicylic acrylate acts as a chelating ligand and can be complexed with Zn(II) for subsequent thermal polymerization with EGDMA.

Apigenin inhibits AGE-promoted inflammation and oxidative stress in human umbilical vein endothelial cells (HUVECs) by suppressing the ERK/NF-β signaling pathway through inhibition of AGE-RAGE interaction.
Dothiorelone K is a cytosporone derivative derived from the fungal endophyte Dothiorella sp. ML002 and exhibits alpha-glucosidase inhibitory activity with an IC50 value of 22.0 µg/ml.
Neonambiterphenyl A is a newly identified p-terphenyl derivative, structurally confirmed by NMR and comparison to boletopsin A, a KDR kinase inhibitor.

(E)-4-(5-bromo-2-hydroxybenzylidene)-6-hydroxy-8-methoxy-3,4-dihydrobenzo[b]oxepin-5(2H)-one is a 3,4-dihydro-1-benzoxepin-5(2H)-one derivative that inhibits protein-tyrosine kinases, with greater effectiveness against ErbB1 and ErbB2 than 6-methoxy-substituted analogues.
At a concentration of 100 µM in aqueous medium, 2-hydroxy-4,6-dimethoxybenzoic acid acts as a pro-oxidant.
6,8-dihydroxy-5-methoxy-3-methyl-1H-isochromen-1-one exhibits α-glucosidase inhibitory activity with an IC50 value of 89.4 µM.
Bellidifolin (1,5,8-trihydroxy-3-methoxyxanthone) is a selective inhibitor of monoamine oxidase A (MAO A).
Bellidifolin exhibits acetylcholinesterase (AChE) inhibitory activity, with potency similar to galanthamine in enzyme assays.
(E)-4-(3,5-dimethoxystyryl)phenyl acetate suppresses the expression of cyclin D1 and cyclin B1 in glioblastoma cells.
A genomic region encoding acetyltransferase activity capable of converting MAPG to 2,4-DAPG has been described for P. fluorescens strain F113.
Rubrofusarin is an aglycone that forms the core structure of the disaccharide compound rubrofusarin-6-13-Dgentiobioside, with a disaccharide moiety attached at the 6-position.
Artocarpetin is a furanoflavone isolated from Artocarpus heterophyllus wood that exhibits strong mushroom tyrosinase inhibitory activity with an IC50 lower than 50 µM.
Artocarpetin is a potent tyrosinase inhibitor that contains a 4-resorcinol moiety in the B ring of its flavone skeleton.
Reticulol inhibits metastasis of B16F10 melanoma cells in mice in a dose-dependent manner, with intravenous administration resulting in greater metastasis suppression than peritonitis administration.
In P. fluorescens strain Q2-87, the products of the 2,4-DAPG biosynthetic genes phlACB are necessary for the conversion of MAPG to 2,4-DAPG.
Echisoside upregulates bax and caspase3 expression and downregulates cyclin D1, bcl2, VEGFA, c-myc, and survivin in AGS cancer cells.
Artocarpetin is a flavone analog with high inhibitory activity against mushroom tyrosinase, which is substantially decreased by methoxylation at the C7 position.
Echisoside induces morphological changes characteristic of apoptotic cell death and causes cell cycle arrest at the G1 phase in AGS cancer cells.
Rubrofusarin, a xanthone, can affect the rate of dehydrogenation of isopropyl alcohol to acetone by Fusarium lini Bolley (FlB).
Echisoside inhibits proliferation and induces apoptosis in AGS (adenocarcinoma gastric) cancer cells in vitro.

In transgenic Alzheimer's disease mouse models, 3-(benzyloxy)-1-(5-[18F]fluoropentyl)-5-nitro-1H-indazole ([18F]-IND1) exhibits differential binding in multiple brain regions compared to normal mice, suggesting region-specific acetylcholinesterase activity changes in Alzheimer's disease.
(+)-2-hydroxyxenylmethylpiperidine is as effective as R( -)-deprenyl in protecting noradrenaline axons against DSP-4 neurotoxicity.
N,N-diphenyl-dopamine analogues do not induce asymmetric postures in caudectomized mice.
The slow plasma metabolism of (S)-(+)-1-(1-methyl-1H-pyrrol-2-yl)-2-phenyl-2-(1-pyrrolidinyl)ethanone may be advantageous for routine PET studies of MAO-A.
α-aminovalerophenone derivatives directly block dopamine uptake in rat brain synaptosomes and transfected HEK293 cells.
1-methyl-4-(3', 4'-dihydroxyphenyl)pyridinium bromide fails to modify dopamine neuronal function in vivo, despite high potency in DHPR inhibition in vitro.
Activation of protein kinase C attenuates muscarinic-induced changes in intracellular calcium levels in SH-SYSY cells.
β-secretase inhibitor IV is a genuine BACE-1 inhibitor.
2-[18F]fluoro-3-(2(S)-azetidinylmethoxy)pyridine crosses the blood-brain barrier and distributes in Rhesus monkey brain in a pattern consistent with alpha4beta2 nAChRs.
Administration of (m-nitrobenzoyl)-alanine increases kynurenic acid concentration in dialysates from gerbils with a dorsal hippocampal dialysis probe or rats with a parietal cortex dialysis probe.
(-)-(2R,3R)-5-(2-[18F]fluoroethoxy)-3-(4-phenylpiperidin-1-yl)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydro-naphthalen-2-ol specifically maps cholinergic areas in vivo.
MPDP+ is a common intermediate from which both MPP+ and MPTP lactam are derived in mouse liver enzyme metabolism of MPTP.
(-)-(2R,3R)-5-(2-[18F]fluoroethoxy)-3-(4-phenylpiperidin-1-yl)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydro-naphthalen-2-ol demonstrates high brain extraction and favorable in vivo metabolism.
Hemicholinium mustard does not significantly affect synaptosomal uptake of dopamine and GABA at a dose that causes substantial HACU inhibition in rat cortical synaptosomes.
N-(anilinomethyl)-p-isopropoxyphenylsuccinimide (AMIPPS) significantly increases total brain valproate concentrations in mice without affecting total brain phenobarbital concentrations.
In APP SW /PS1 dE9 transgenic mice, 2-[(pyridine-2-ylmethyl)-amino]-phenol ameliorates Aβ pathology and rescues memory deficits.
R-(+)-3-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-N-propylpiperidine hydrochloride does not significantly alter NMDA-induced calcium flux in rat cortical neurons.
R-(+)-3-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-N-propylpiperidine hydrochloride, a sigma-acting compound, does not alter methamphetamine-induced neurotoxicity in mice.
M344 modulates several AD-related pathways and attenuates cognitive decline in a chronically treated AD mouse model.
NSD 2023 is a more effective inhibitor of mouse brain MAO in vivo than in vitro.
During N-pentyl-N-isopropylbenzamide treatment, one patient experienced a hyperammonemic crisis and another withdrew due to high ammonia and headache.
2-(2-methylquinolin-4-ylamino)-N-phenylacetamide exhibits strong antileishmanial activity against L. donovani in both in vitro and in vivo assays.
The stereochemistry of 2-phenyl-1-propanol formation from cumene metabolism by CYP4B1 provides insight into the enzyme-substrate interaction during catalysis.
1-Benzoylbenztriazole inhibits electron transport in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Bencain at concentrations of 0.19 mM and 0.38 mM shifts the resting potential of nerve nodes by 3–6 mV toward hyperpolarization.

Bencain at a concentration of 0.19 mM reduces the amplitude and steepness of the action potential (AP) and increases the critical depolarization level (CDL) in nerve nodes, while at 0.38 mM it abolishes AP generation and causes local responses to strong stimuli.
The ethyl nicotinate complex 2n is at least 8 times more active than cisplatin against L1210/R cells.
Bencain at 0.38 mM completely prevents action potential generation in Ranvier nodes and slightly increases the initial resting potential by 3–6 mV.
Biphenylacetic acid only weakly enhances the inhibitory effect of levofloxacin at GABA receptors in mice.
Pyridoxine tribenzoate retains biological activity as a vitamin B6 supplement, as demonstrated by a single-dose assay with acrodynic rats.
α-aminovalerophenone derivatives directly block dopamine uptake in rat brain synaptosomes and transfected HEK293 cells.
Bupropion hydrochloride inhibits inflammatory cytokine production.

Glycerol benzoate at concentrations of 100 or 200 mM significantly increases the percentage of motile spermatozoa stored for 48 h at 0°C and protects spermatozoa stored at 20° or 5°C, improving motility after 96 h incubation.
2-fluoroacetophenone is converted to its corresponding phenyl acetate via HAPMO-catalyzed biotransformation by whole cells of P. fluorescens ACB.

Bulky halide substituents at the 4-position of the phenylbutyrophenone moiety greatly improve affinity for the DA D 2 receptor and reduce 5-HT 2A affinity by approximately 35-fold, resulting in a reversed selectivity profile similar to classical neuroleptics.
Introduction of a 2-amino group onto the phenylbutyrophenone scaffold enhances binding affinity at all three target receptors (5-HT 2A, 5-HT 2C, and DA D 2) compared to the parent compound.
Phenylbutyrophenone derivatives function as antagonists at the 5-HT 2A, 5-HT 2C, and DA D 2 receptors, with selectivity profiles influenced by substituents at the phenyl ring.
HSD can scavenge and inactivate harmful radical species such as alkyl peroxyl radicals (E = 1.05 V) and superoxide radicals (E = 0.94 V) in the organism.
Glycerol benzoate acts in a temperature- and concentration-dependent manner to eliminate male-to-male variability in sperm motility during cryopreservation.
N-methyl-phenylcarbamate can partially inhibit the regrowth of colistin-resistant subpopulations in Pseudomonas aeruginosa and Stenotrophomonas maltophilia.
furan-2-ylfuranopyrimidine retains strong inhibitory activity against EGFR when substituted at the furanopyrimidine core.
The anticancer activity of di-2-pyridylketone dithiocarbamate (DpdtC) against hepatoma cell lines is partly attributed to ferritinophagy-mediated lysosomal reactive oxygen species (ROS) generation.
2-bromo-3-(furan-2-yl)-3-oxo-propionamide exhibits MIC50 values of 6.25 µg/mL against Pseudomonas aeruginosa ATCC 27853 and 12.50 µg/mL against Escherichia coli ATCC 27853.

Di-2-pyridylketone dithiocarbamate (DpdtC) is a novel iron chelator that can mobilize iron from ferritin and displays antitumor activity against hepatoma carcinoma cell lines, with IC50 values of 0.4 ± 0.2 µM for HepG2 and 3.5 ± 0.3 µM for Bel-7402.
Titanocene bis(p-aminothiophenolate) bis(hydrochloride) shows moderate antitumor activity against human lung adenocarcinoma and small cell lung carcinoma in xenograft models.
Imidazoquinolinedione derivatives have been developed as potential anticancer agents.
Furanyl salicylate acts as a phosphotyrosine mimetic but is much less polar than phosphotyrosine and functions as a competitive inhibitor of Yersinia PTP YopH.
Phenylacetamide-1H-imidazol-5-one (KIM-161) induces cell cycle exit and apoptosis in HL60 cells independently of tubulin or Src kinase inhibition.
Nexturastat A inhibits the growth of B16 melanoma cells and demonstrates antiproliferative effects.
Furanyl salicylate derivatives with IC50 values of 180 nM, 260 nM, 390 nM, and 460 nM were identified as potent inhibitors of Yersinia PTP YopH in in silico docking studies.
N-Hydroxy-4-(4-phenylbutyryl-amino) benzamide induces cell cycle arrest and mitochondria-mediated apoptosis in lung cancer cells.
Metal complexes of o-hydroxydithiobenzoate may have a dual mechanism of action in tumor regression, potentially reversing tumor-associated immunosuppression.
Erasin is a RAS-selective lethal compound that induces ferroptosis.
2-(2-furoyl)-4(5)-(2-furanyl)-1H-imidazole is an advanced protein glycation product that can be detected by fluorescence at 440 nm in plasma peptide analysis.
Dipyridylketone 4,4-dimethylthiosemicarbazone (Dp44mT) is highly effective in vivo against a panel of human tumor xenografts and cultured tumor cells in vitro.
Toluenesulfonyloxy substitution at C-2 of 1,4-naphthoquinone increases cytotoxicity against cancer cell lines compared to unsubstituted analogues.
2-(2-furoyl)-4(5)-(2-furanyl)-1H-imidazole is an advanced glycation end product (AGE) that binds to a macrophage receptor and is internalized by macrophages.
2-bromo-3-(furan-2-yl)-3-oxopropionamide is a novel antibacterial compound discovered through virtual screening and 2.5D-QSAR modeling.
Gemcitabine is a novel antimetabolite with broad activity in preclinical solid tumor models.
Furoyl pyrroloquinolone is a highly selective and potent inhibitor of the phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) enzyme.
Benzene porphyrin is used as a component in second generation domestic photosensitive agents for the treatment of digestive tract tumors, contributing to their photosensitive and curative properties.
Methyl{6-chloro-5-[(4-chloro-1-naphthyl)oxy]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl}carbamate produces complete absence of retinal blood vessels in zebrafish embryos at the highest tested concentration.

Silver(II) porphyrin dimers can undergo stepwise oxidation to generate both AgII /AgIII mixed-valent and AgIII /AgIII porphyrin dimers, with 2e oxidation stabilizing the cis form due to metallophilic interaction.
2-(2-Hydroxy-5-tert-butylphenyl)benzotriazole (BP-3) at low concentrations inhibits steroidogenesis and alters the hormonal milieu in zebrafish during different developmental stages.
Upon removal of two electrons from the silver(II) porphyrin dimer, a mixed-valent pi-cation radical species is formed, with its structure influenced by the nature of counter ions.

Nickel porphyrin forms complexes with mutant and wild type ferrochelatase, and the resonance Raman spectrum of Ni(ProtoP) in the E287Q mutant shows spectral features with frequencies nearly identical to those of Ni(MesoP) in solution at pH 8.
Silver(II) porphyrin can form monometallic dimers with a highly flexible ethane bridge, which enables stepwise electron removal from the metal center during oxidation.
Chromium deuteroporphyrin does not significantly affect HO-1 expression in HO-1-luc15 cells.
silver(II) porphyrin is a water-soluble porphyrin complex with potential for photodynamic therapy and antibacterial applications due to its unique photophysical and chemical properties.
Hydroxy-iron(III) porphyrin can participate in hydrogen atom transfer and recombination reactions with etodolac radicals in the presence of oxo-iron(IV) porphyrins.
Chromium deutero porphyrin lowers HO-1 expression in NIH 3T3 cells compared to untreated controls.

CrDP is a metalloporphyrin that acts as a nonphotosensitizing inhibitor of heme oxygenase (HO) and is considered a promising candidate for antihyperbilirubinemic drug development due to its high potency and low photoreactivity.
Bis(2,2,4-trifluorophenyl) ether (BDE-47) significantly alters thyroid hormone regulation in zebrafish embryo-larvae.
FeTPP(OH)4 catalyzes the oxidation and dechlorination of certain organic pollutants in soil suspensions, achieving up to 60–62% degradation in allophanic and red soil suspensions.
Porphyrin tin dichloride is a volatile compound that constitutes the main component of the vapour over a sample during GED experiments as the temperature approaches 700 K.
PbB is a urinary biomarker used to assess lead exposure, and its levels show a significant curvilinear relationship with PbU-EDTA over a broad range of PbB values.
Zinc(II) bis-porphyrin forms complexes with cationic bis-porphyrins and can be used to study DNA-protein interactions in gel electrophoresis assays.
The second reduction of (P)SnCl2 is irreversible at room temperature and follows an electrochemical electron transfer mechanism, with electroactive products formed.
The monoanion of (PQ)SnCl2 is stable on the cyclic voltammetry time scale but reacts on the slower time scale of thin-layer spectroelectrochemistry.
Porphyrin tin(II) chloride can be linked to a synergistic mitochondrial Ca2+ overload strategy to enhance antitumor effects when combined with calcium carbonate-based nanoparticles.
2,3,4-trihydroxy-3',4'-dimethoxydeoxybenzoin displays potent mushroom tyrosinase inhibitory activity with IC50 values of 43.37, 43.10, and 46.10 µM at 0.5, 1.5, and 2.5 hours, respectively.

The 1H and 15N chemical shifts of the bound ligands in DHFR complexes with brodimoprim analogues indicate that the 2,4-diaminopyrimidine and benzyl ring adopt essentially the same binding site and conformation in different complexes.
N'-(5-bromo-2-oxoindolin-3-ylidene)-3,4,5-trimethoxybenzohydrazide is an initial screening hit compound that induces apoptosis in human colorectal carcinoma HCT116 cells.

N,N,N′,N′-Tetrakis(5,6,7-trimethoxy-2-quinolylmethyl)ethylenediamine (T(TMQ)EN) is used to treat PC-12 rat adrenal pheochromocytoma cells for 4 hours at a concentration of 50 µM in RPMI 1640 media supplemented with 5% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin.

The 2,4-diaminopyrimidine and benzyl ring moieties of brodimoprim bind in essentially the same binding sites and with the same overall conformation in Lactobacillus casei dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR) complexes.

6,7-dihydroxy-5,3',4',5'-tetramethoxy isoflavone is a metabolite of irisflorentin detected in rat liver microsomes and is characterized by a sigmoidal enzymatic kinetic profile (Vmax = 12.02 µM/mg protein/min, Km = 37.24 µM, CLint = 0.32 µL/mg protein/min, h = 1.48) indicating positive cooperation.
2,3,4-trihydroxy-3',4'-dimethoxydeoxybenzoin shows strong antioxidant activity, outperforming Trolox and vitamin C in DPPH radical scavenging assays.
The formation of 6,7-dihydroxy-5,3',4',5'-tetramethoxy isoflavone from irisflorentin is primarily catalyzed by cytochrome P450 enzymes CYP1A2 and CYP3A4.

Brodimoprim analogues with carboxylated alkyl chains at the 3′-O position are designed to improve binding to Lactobacillus casei dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR) by enhancing interactions with basic residues in the protein.
3alpha,6beta-di-(3,4,5-trimethoxycinnamoyloxy)tropane is a tropane alkaloid aromatic ester that restores vinblastine sensitivity in multidrug-resistant KB-VI cells with an IC50 of 2.0–3.7 µM.
5,4′-dihydroxy-7,8,2′,3′tetramethoxyflavone shows the least synergistic effect with cypermethrin toxicity among the tested flavones in CYP6AA3-expressing cells.

Brodimoprim analogues with carboxylated alkyl chains at the 3'-O position are designed to improve binding to Lactobacillus casei dihydrofolate reductase by enhancing interactions with basic protein residues.

5,4'-dihydroxy-7,8,2′,3′-tetramethoxyflavone, a 4′-hydroxylated derivative of 5-hydroxy-7,8,2′,3′-tetramethoxyflavone, decreases the Ki of CYP6AA3 from 4.73 to 2.32 µM but increases the Ki of CYP6P7 from 5.59 to 11.01 µM.
Papaverine sulfate (PSA) is entrapped in the lipid bilayer of liposomes, and its release rate is influenced by the surface area and charge of the liposomes.

5,4′-dihydroxy-7,8,2′,3′-tetramethoxyflavone is a methoxylated flavonoid that acts as a mechanism-based, time- and concentration-dependent, irreversible inhibitor of CYP6AA3 in a mixed-type (competitive and noncompetitive) mode, with an inactivation efficiency (k inact/K i) of 44.44 min⁻¹ mM⁻¹.

3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl-4-(2-oxoimidazolidin-1-yl)benzenesulfonate, also known as IMZ-602, is a tubulin-binding drug that inhibits the growth of tumor-derived cell lines with IC50 values below 10 nM and disrupts the tubulin cytoskeleton at 4 nM (immunofluorescence of ␤-tubulin in M21 cells).
V04 strongly inhibits the activity of partially purified pancreatic aminopeptidase (PAP) toward phosphoaminoacids, showing 95% inhibition of AP activity when p-tyr is used as substrate.

3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl-4-(2-oxoimidazolidin-1-yl)benzenesulfonate (IMZ-602), a combretastatin A-4 mimetic and tubulin ligand, causes condensation of B2R-GFP, fluorescein-5-thiocarbamoyl-D-Arg-[Hyp3, Igl5, Oic7, Igl8]-BK, and beta-arrestin2-GFP into punctate structures close to the cell surface.

Dihydroferulic (3-(4-hydroxy-3-methoxyphenyl) propionic acid) is present at the highest concentration in 'Nitric-extract' and shows significant inhibitory activity against Fusarium oxysporum and Penicillium italicum.

4-amino-2-hydroxy-5-(3',4',5'-trimethoxybenzyl)pyrimidine is a degradation product of trimethoprim, with retention times of -4.9 min (mobile phase for solid dosage forms) and -10.0 min (mobile phase for oral suspension) by HPLC.
Nicotinamide isonicotinate (NIC) can hydrotroically solubilize 1,6-Bis(1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)hexane, enhancing its permeation into deeper skin layers when applied percutaneously.
N-(2-morpholinoethyl)nicotinamide derivatives are potent and selective inhibitors of monoamine oxidase A (MAO-A) over MAO-B.
Nicotinic azide has some pharmacological effects.

The cytotoxic activity of 2,4,4-trimethoxychalcone derivatives against K562 cells is reduced 2-7 fold when a 3-prenyl or 3-geranyl moiety is present at the 3-position compared to their trihydroxy counterparts.
5-O-desosaminylerythronolide A oxime is slightly more active than the corresponding 9-ketone derivative against the tested activity, with its potency expressed as a pKO,%, value.

Isopropyl nicotinate is released from Viscoleo into an aqueous acetate buffer at pH 5.00 and 37°C, with a higher release rate than methyl and ethyl nicotinate but lower than butyl nicotinate in this system.
Sporiolide A is a 12-membered lactone fungal metabolite isolated from Cladosporium sp. cultured from Actinotrichia fragilis and Niphates rowi.
Isopropyl nicotinate lowers liver fat content in both rats and chicks.
Ethyl nicotinate is more sensitive to hydrolysis than butyl nicotinate in acidic solution at pH 1.15 and 60°C.
Sporiolide A displays antibacterial activity against Micrococcus luteus with a MIC of 16.7 μg/mL.
The chemical structure of sporiolide A was proposed in 2004 to correspond to 3-O-benzoyl pandangolide 1 based on spectroscopic data.
Ethyl nicotinate can be efficiently utilized as a substrate in transglycosidation reactions, requiring only a modest excess and subsequent hydrolysis to form NAADP.
The ethyl nicotinate complex 2n is at least 8 times more active than cisplatin against L1210/R cells.
Nicotinic acid isopropyl ester binds to human plasma lipoproteins such as LDL, IDL, and HDL, as analyzed by equilibrium dialysis and Sephadex gel filtration.
A y(CH2)-value of 0.55 has been determined for ethyl nicotinate in an octanol/water partitioning system.
Sporiolide A shows antifungal activity with MIC values of 16.7 μg/mL against Candida albicans, 8.4 μg/mL against Cryptococcus neoformans, and Aspergillus niger, and Neurospora crassa.
Sporiolide A exhibits cytotoxicity against L1210 cells with an IC50 value of 0.13 μg/mL.
The stability of ethyl nicotinate was studied in buffer solutions at pH 1.15 and 60°C.
The presence of a 3-pyridyl group is essential for a high degree and consistent coronary dilating action in heterocyclic-substituted acrylophenones.

Esulatin D (compound 382) is a jatrophane polyester isolated from Euphorbia esula that exhibits weak potency in reversing multidrug resistance in L5178 mouse lymphoma cells, with a fluorescence activity ratio (FAR) between 2.60 and 2.79 at 11.2 µM.
5-(pyridin-4-yl)-3H-1,3,4-oxadiazol-2-thione derivatives may interact at the active site of mycobacterial cytochrome P450-dependent sterol 14demethylase in the sterol biosynthesis pathway.
4H-pyridopyrimidine analogs exhibit broad-spectrum inhibition of bacterial growth.
5-(pyridine-4-yl)-1,3,4-oxadiazole-2(3H)-thione serves as a core structure for the synthesis of oxadiazole derivatives evaluated as antimycobacterial agents.
2-acetamido-4-methylthiazole can be administered orally to male Wistar rats at a mean dose of 100 mg/kg body weight, with recovery of radioactivity measured over 0–24 hours.
Methoxyimino cephem derivatives exhibit good oral absorbability, attributed to the N,IV-dimethylcarbamoyloxy group at the C-3 position.
2-methoxy-1,3-thiazole 10d displays high clearance and low bioavailability in rats.
Nitrofuryltriazole is a compound whose oral absorption and urinary excretion have been tested in dogs, with total urinary excretion measured over 0–6 hours after a single 5 mg/kg oral dose.
Thiazolo[4,5-d]thiazolidine-2-thione serves as a scaffold for the synthesis of pyrazoline derivatives with demonstrated cytotoxicity and multitargeting kinase inhibitory activity.
Cephalosporins bearing 2-amino-1-(3-methylamino-propyl)-1H-imidazo[4,5-b]pyridinium as the C-3' side chain exhibit high stability against AmpC beta-lactamase in vivo.
The intestinal permeability of 6-fluoroquinolone derivatives increases with lipophilicity up to a certain limit, after which permeability may decrease depending on the number of substituents.
5-(pyridin-4-yl)-3H-1,3,4-oxadiazol-2-thione derivatives have been synthesized and tested for in vitro antimycobacterial activity against M. tuberculosis H37Rv.
5-(pyridin-4-yl)-3H-1,3,4-oxadiazol-2-thione derivatives exhibit in vitro antimycobacterial activity against M. tuberculosis H 37 Rv strain.
A key 5-(1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-1,2,4-oxadiazole compound demonstrates a favorable pharmacokinetic profile in rats after intravenous and oral administration.
In dogs, 5H-[1,3,4]thiadiazolo[3,2-a]pyrimidin-5-one demonstrates rapid oral absorption (Tmax 0.5 hr), high oral bioavailability (~92%), and rapid elimination (t1/2 2 hr).
Chloroaminothiazole is a chemical group that, when incorporated into a siderophore-conjugated g-lactam antibiotic analog, favorably modulates several biological properties.
Bismerthiazol is a fungicide tested for growth inhibition in green algae Chlorella pyrenoidosa and Scenedesmus obliqnus.
Bismerthiazol is classified as a thyroid disruptor.
Bismerthiazol can influence the synthesis of thyroxin by restraining the synthesis of the protein prohormone Tg.

Bismerthiazol administration increases thyroid weight, alters thyroid hormone levels by increasing TSH and decreasing serum T3 and T4, and induces changes such as colloid depletion and follicular cell hypertrophy in the thyroid.

Bismerthiazol exposure during development is likely a thyroid disruptor in female rats, as evidenced by decreased serum triiodothyronine (T3) and thyroxine (T4) concentrations, increased thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH) concentrations, and thyroid gland hyperplasia.
8-methoxy-3-phenylcoumarin is a highly selective MAO-B inhibitor.
2-methoxyphenethyl alcohol is the aglycone obtained by treatment of tazettoside C with 1 M HCl.
5,7-dimethoxy-4-phenylcoumarin mildly but significantly reduces the formation of TNF-alpha in LPS-induced murine macrophage RAW 264.7 cells.
5,7-dimethoxy-4-phenylcoumarin is used as a test compound in cell viability assays, with its effect on cell growth measured by MTT reduction and expressed as IC50.
2-methoxyphenethyl alcohol is produced as a product during acid hydrolysis of 2-methoxyphenethyl b-Dglucopyranoside with 1 M HCl.
4-methoxy-6-phenyl-1H-indazole exhibits good HDAC inhibitory activity and potent cellular anti-proliferative activity.
2',5'-dimethoxychalcone derivatives exhibit low micromolar in vitro activity against several human cancer cell lines.
2-(3′,4′,5′-Trimethoxybenzoyl)-3-amino-5-phenyl thiophene exhibits antiproliferative activity and induces G2/M cell cycle arrest and apoptosis in HL-60 cells.
2-(2-methoxyphenyl)ethanol inhibits mushroom tyrosinase activity by 12.7% ± 0.7% at a concentration of 4 mM.
The 3′-hydroxy-4′-methoxy derivative of flavonol exhibits higher cytotoxicity than the 3′-hydroxy-4′-methoxy derivative in HL-60, U-937, and MOLT-3 human leukaemia cell lines.
4-ethyl-2-phenylphenol is included in the estrogenic group mixture of endocrine-disrupting chemicals (EDCs) that exhibit estrogenic effects at environmentally relevant concentrations.

The cyclization step for 2-methoxyphenethyl alcohol requires an activating group para to the cyclization site, as the cyclization proceeds poorly in its absence due to inductive deactivation of the ortho-methoxy group.
Trp-171 in LiP is a key residue for the oxidation of VA (vanillin aldehyde), but not for lignin binding.

5-hydroxymethyl-2-hydroxy-4-methoxy-phenylethanone is a newly isolated compound from Craterellus odoratus that has been evaluated for inhibitory activity against human 11b-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase (11b-HSD1) and cytotoxic activity on five human cancer cell lines.
4-ethyl-2-phenylphenol is a phenolic endocrine disruptor that can be detected in human urine at relatively high concentrations due to its metabolic stability and excretion as metabolites.
A 2,5-dimethoxyphenyl unit on the ring C substituent of butenolide derivatives confers cytotoxic activity similar to that of a 2,5-dimethoxyphenol derivative.

4-ethyl-2-phenylphenol acts as an inducer of CYP102 in Bacillus megaterium, and its induction is additive with that of 100 lM E2 at the same concentration and time points, indicating action at a different regulatory site than E2.
The number of sorption isotherms for 4-ethyl-2-phenylphenol onto various biochars is generally inversely correlated with the CO2-SA/OC ratio, supporting the role of pore-filling in its adsorption.

The sorption of 4-ethyl-2-phenylphenol onto wood-derived biochars (WDBs) shows a positive correlation between logKoc and the CO2-SA/OC ratio, indicating that pore-filling is a significant mechanism for its adsorption.
2-phenyl-4-methoxyphenol, when combined with a hydroxyl group at position 2 and a methoxy group at position 3, achieves a final mortality rate of 70.66% in enzyme inhibition assays.
trans-1,4-bis[[1-cyclohexyl-3-(4-dimethylamino phenyl) ureido]methyl]cyclohexane (NTE-122) is a novel acyl-CoA:cholesterol acyltransferase (ACAT) inhibitor.
Among the oxygenated sterols in LDL, 4␤-OH-Chol is not among those that show high potency in lowering HMG-CoA reductase activity.
Enzymatic hydrolysis of cis-2-hydroxy-4-phenyl-4-butyrolactone can yield optically pure (2S)-cis-lactone within a comparable timeframe to trans-2-hydroxy-4-phenyl-4-butyrolactone.

(3S*,4R*)-(3-hydroxy-4-phenylazetidin-2-one) is used for enzymatic resolution and can be enantioselectively ring cleaved by CAL-B lipase to yield β-amino acid (2R,3S)-20 with enantiomeric excess greater than 98% and yield greater than 45%.
1-(4-Pyrimidinyl)-4-(1-(4-bromophenylsulfonyl)piperazin-4-ylcarbonyl)piperidine is an inhibitor of cholesterol biosynthesis, as demonstrated by its effect on hepatic lipid profiles in rats.
Picolinoylpyrrolidine-2-carboxylic acid serves as a moiety in the design of DGAT-1 inhibitors targeting hyperlipidemia and metabolic disorders.

5j (sodium (3R,5R)-7-(3-(N,N-dimethylsulfamoyl)-5-(4-fluorophenyl)-2-isopropyl-4phenyl-1H-pyrrol-1-yl)-3,5-dihydroxyheptanoate) is a 4-sulfamoyl pyrrole statin derivative that inhibits HMGCR with an affinity value of -12.17 kcal/mol and binding energy of -94.10 kcal/mol via five hydrogen bonds.
Ceestatin exerts antiviral effects against HCV by inhibiting HMG-CoA synthase.
(R)-3,5-dihydroxyheptanoate is the active side chain in statins that inhibits 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl coenzyme A reductase, a key enzyme in cholesterol biosynthesis.
5-(tetradecyloxy)-2-furoic acid (TOFA) inhibits fatty acid and cholesterol synthesis in rat hepatocytes.
Ezetimibe selectively inhibits the intestinal absorption of cholesterol-related phytosterols.
Trimethylamine carboxyborane blocks regulator enzyme activities in the hepatic de novo synthesis of cholesterol.
(2R)-7-(3-[2-chloro-4-(4-fluorophenoxy)phenoxy]propoxy)-2-ethylchromane-2-carboxylic acid is a potent, structurally novel, selective PPARalpha/gamma dual agonist.

Allyl[6-[4-(4-bromophenyl)-2H-chromen-7-yloxy]hexyl]methylamine inhibits cholesterol synthesis in primary rat hepatocytes, with its efficacy and potency determined by measuring residual inhibition after 2, 8, or 24 h incubation.
3,4-dihydroxyhydrocinnamate lowers hepatic HMG-CoA reductase activity and hepatic cholesterol in rats fed a high cholesterol diet.
3-N-(1',8'-Naphthalimido)propionic acid does not inhibit HMG CoA reductase, the regulatory enzyme of cholesterol synthesis.
Ceestatin is a novel inhibitor of HCV replication that also inhibits HMG-CoA synthase.

The hydrolysis of cholesterol carbonate can be catalyzed by imprinted polymer catalysts, resulting in a 27-fold enhancement over uncatalyzed reaction and a 2.4-fold improvement compared to control polymer.

5-[3-[3-(4-phenoxy-2-propylphenoxy)-propoxy]-phenyl]-2,4-thiazolidinedione sodium salt is a dual alpha/gamma peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor (PPAR) agonist with potential use in diabetic patients.

5-[3-(3-(4-phenoxy-2-propylphenoxy)propoxy)phenyl]-2,4-thiazolidinedione sodium salt is a dual ␣/␥ peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor (PPAR) agonist with potential use in the treatment of diabetic patients.
In rats, the rate of transport of [14C]-D-lactate into liver in vivo is 1.8 µmol·min⁻¹·g⁻¹, with a volume of distribution of 0.71 ± 0.07 mL/g and an extraction of 91% ± 9% for unidirectional influx.
6-methylpyrimidine retains in vitro activity against T. brucei rhodesiense.
The concentration of U:7-C dapsone in compartment 2 does not significantly differ in diabetic erythrocytes when DAPS is present in compartment 1.
D-phenylalanyl-D-alanine is a poor substrate for both transport and hydrolysis by the rat small intestine.
Serum iron in mice can be measured using the ferrous triazine colorimetric method, with real-time absorption spectra reflecting iron concentration.
[1-13C]2-deoxyglucose can be used to measure blood lactate labeling in mice via primed infusion, enabling assessment of metabolic labeling in vivo.

Tetrafluorophenoxy methyl ketone analogues exhibit dose-dependent reduction of liver injury endpoints in an R-Fas-mediated model, with ED50 values in the mg/kg range depending on administration route.
D-phenylalanyl-D-alanine is not detected in the mucosa after perfusion through the lumen, indicating minimal paracellular transport in the tested rat jejunum preparation.
The liver plays a significant role in the clearance of 2,4-dinitrophenyl-N-acetylcysteine (DNP-NAC) from the bloodstream.
(R)-4-(Methylnitrosamino)-1-(3-pyridyl)-1-butanol is preferentially metabolically activated in mouse lung compared to other enantiomers.
1-(4-chlorophenyl)-4,4,4-trifluoro-3-hydroxy-2-buten-1-one enhances antibacterial activity at acidic pH by lowering bacterial intracellular pH.

Liposomes containing 2-(4-((1,5-bis(octadecenoyl)1,5-dioxopentan-2-yl)carbamoyl) pyridin-1-ium-1-yl)acetate (DCPA-H2O) become protonated below pH 6.8, enabling fast self-targeting to infectious biofilms after tail-vein injection in mice.
2-methoxy-1,3-thiazole 10d displays high clearance and low bioavailability in rats.
The conversion of Hydroquinone-glutathione to a di-glutathione conjugate in hepatocytes requires rate constants that are optimized to account for mass balance in metabolic studies.
Nitrosoisocytosines are potent synthase inhibitors but lack significant antibacterial activity at 100 pg/mL in vitro.
Chloroaminothiazole is a chemical group that, when incorporated into a siderophore-conjugated g-lactam antibiotic analog, favorably modulates several biological properties.

1,2,3-triazolylmethylene derivatives of 6-(substituted methylene)penem are potent inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamases and were selected for further evaluation based on their stability in human kidney homogenate, binding to human serum, and activity in a mouse infection model.
[14C] acetanilide is a precursor of acetaminophen in rat liver, and its hepatic intrinsic clearance influences the extent of acetaminophen sulfation in the liver.
Iodonicotinic acid, when used as a catabolite in radioiodinated peptides, clears faster from most organs in normal mice compared to iodobenzoic acid.
An intact 3-(2-aminopyridinyl)acrylamide subunit is the minimum key pharmacophore required for FabI activity and antibacterial activity.
7-benzyloxychromone derivatives exhibit moderate inhibition of MAO-B, with the IC50 value for MAO-B inhibition being approximately 0.018 µM.
6-amino-penicillanic acid benzyl ester is used as a coupling partner in the synthesis of both triprotected and [6alpha-3H]-labeled penicillin N derivatives.
Attachment of a 3-(phenoxymethyl)phenol substituent to the phenyl ring in derivative 4g confers comparable inhibitory potency to compound 4e, with an IC50 of 0.23 ± 0.08 µM against rat e5'NT.

7-benzyloxychromone derivatives with meta, para, or trifluoromethyl substitution on the benzyloxy phenyl ring are approximately two- to sixfold more potent MAO-B inhibitors than 7-benzyloxychromone (IC50 values ranging from 0.014 to 0.044 µM).
3,4-di-O-benzyl-D-glucal can function as a glucal donor in one-pot oxidative glycosylation to generate disaccharides via reaction with hydroxy nucleophiles.

3-(carbobenzoxyamino)-4,7-dihydroxy-8-methylcoumarin is a new inhibitor of Micrococcus luteus DNA gyrase and exhibits weak antibacterial activity against whole M. luteus cells, likely due to poor cell envelope penetration.
The metagenomic carboxylesterase IS11 shows a strong preference for benzyl (R)-(+)-2-hydroxy-3-phenylpropionate among tested ester substrates.
The 4-(benzyloxy)-3,5-dimethoxybenzoic acid side chain (A52) at the R A position provides potent inhibition of Plm II.

7-benzyloxychromone is a highly potent reversible, competitive inhibitor of human MAO-B, with all tested substitutions and substitution patterns on the benzyloxy ring enhancing its MAO-B inhibition potency.

4-(benzyloxy)-3,5-dimethoxybenzoic acid, when incorporated as side chain A52 in inhibitor 3, confers significantly improved inhibitory activity against Plm II aspartyl protease, with a Ki of 100 nM, which is 2-3 fold better than initial lead compounds.
5-benzyloxyisoflavone analogs exhibit lower inhibitory activity against IL-5 bioactivity compared to their cyclohexylmethoxy analogs.

Tetraethylene glycol monobenzyl ether can be used as a reactant in the transesterification with polyethylene glycols to produce halo-ester-functionalized poly(ethylene glycol)s under solventless conditions using Candida antarctica lipase B as a biocatalyst.
The substitution pattern at the meta or para position of 7-benzyloxychromone derivatives has little effect on their MAO-B inhibitory potency.

2-O-octyl-L-ascorbic acid is a monosubstituted ascorbic acid derivative that shows significant antigen-stimulated degranulation inhibitory activity in RBL-2H3 cells, with its activity depending on alkyl chain length.
2-O-octylascorbic acid, an active oxygen scavenger, reduces the bladder tumor promoting effect of tryptophan.
2-deoxy-2-fluoro-α-D-glucopyranosyl dibenzyl phosphate does not function as a covalent inactivator of its cognate α-glycosidase.
6-benzyloxysalicylate acts as a competitive inhibitor of h-15-LOX-1.
6-benzyloxysalicylate derivatives are h15-LOX-1 inhibitors with modest potency of 7100 nM.
6-benzyloxysalicylate is a structurally novel, competitive inhibitor of h-15-LOX-1 with a Ki value of 1.7 mM.

3-(Benzyloxy)-4-hydroxy-5-(2-hydroxyethyl)-2(5H)-furanone is a compound used as a reference in electrophoretic mobility shift assays (EMSA) to assay NF-κB nuclear translocation in transformed rat alveolar macrophages (NR8383 cells).

The aminolysis of p-nitrophenyl N-methyl-N-phenylcarbamate is at least 10,000 times slower than the aminolysis of p-nitrophenyl N-phenylcarbamate due to the unfeasibility of the isocyanate mechanism when N-H is replaced by N-CH3.

Cyclopropyl p-nitrophenyl ketone is used as a substrate in the nucleophilic aromatic substitution synthesis of no-carrier-added fluorine-18-labeled N-methylspiroperidol, providing a 10–15% radiochemical yield (based on [18F-] with mass <2 nmol and specific activity >10 Ci/mumol (EOB)).
4-(4-nitrophenyl)-5-methoxycarbonyl-6-methyl-3,4-dihydropyridone exhibits potent inhibitory activity against Jack bean urease with an IC50 of 29.12 µM, outperforming hydroxyurea (IC50 = 100.0 µM).

4-nitrobenzylcarbamate prodrugs exhibit high potency against NTR-transfected cells via nitroreductase-mediated bioreductive activation, but require oxygen for the cytotoxicity of the released enediyne effectors, especially in repair-competent cells.
SCH-527123 inhibits proliferation, migration, and invasion, and enhances apoptosis in melanoma cancer cell lines.
1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy) phenyl] propane-1-one (NPPP) is significantly active against Toxoplasma gondii tachyzoites both in vitro and in vivo.
Dantrolene Na at 10 µM blocks caffeine-induced intracellular Ca2+ transients in rat sympathetic neurons, but the inhibition is time-dependent rather than use-dependent.
4-pyridinylcarbamate derivatives of alpha-GalCer have been developed to improve the Th1-polarized antigenic profile when the 6'-OH is solvent-exposed.
Delayed addition of AOPP during the avirulence gene-dependent oxidative burst inhibits H2O2 accumulation within 1 to 2 hours in P. s. glycinea.
2',5'-dimethoxychalcone derivatives exhibit low micromolar in vitro activity against several human cancer cell lines.

Proteomic analysis suggests that 1-[4-(4-nitrophenoxy)phenyl]propane-1-one (NPPP) targets proteins such as a putative vacuolar ATP synthase subunit C and a class I S-adenosylmethionine-dependent methyltransferase in Toxoplasma gondii.

Ethyl 8-(4-methoxyphenyl)-4-oxo-6,7-dihydroimidazo[2,1-c][1,2,4]triazine-3-carboxylate (EIMTC) induces cell cycle arrest in S phase in HeLa, TOV112D, T47D, and Vero cells, and in G2/M phase in TOV112D cells.
SEC-mediated activation of AnxA7 GTPase effectively inhibits prostate cancer metastasis in vivo by suppressing mTORC1/STAT3 signaling and the expression of pro-metastatic genes.

ethyl 8-(4-methoxyphenyl)-4-oxo-6,7-dihydroimidazo[2,1-c][1,2,4]triazine-3-carboxylate exhibits significant antiproliferative activity by inhibiting different phases of the cell cycle and inducing higher rates of necrotic cell death in malignant cells compared to normal epithelial cells.
Pemrametostat is a PRMT5 inhibitor that enhances the efficacy of Palbociclib in CDK-i resistant melanoma cells and delays the onset of CDK-i resistance in naive cells.
N-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)-3-methoxybenzamide arrests melanocyte and melanoma cell proliferation in the G1 phase of the cell cycle and moderately suppresses tumor growth in vivo.
Nexturastat A is a selective HDAC6 inhibitor that, in combination with PD-1 immune blockade, significantly improves anti-tumor immune responses and reduces tumor growth in melanoma models.

Ethyl 8-(4-methoxyphenyl)-4-oxo-6,7-dihydroimidazo[2,1-c][1,2,4]triazine-3-carboxylate (EIMTC) inhibits the growth of malignant multiple myeloma cells, including those resistant to thalidomide, at micromolar concentrations with less cytotoxicity on normal HSF cells.
In MCF-7 cells, 2-(3,4-dimethoxyphenyl)-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-thiazolidin-4-one can interfere with the estrogen receptor α (ERα)-dependent pathway.
2-(3,4-dimethoxyphenyl)-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-thiazolidin-4-one displays potent cytotoxic activity against MCF-7 and SKBR3 breast cancer cells.
Amidox is a novel anticancer drug that undergoes extensive metabolism in rat liver, with about 55% of applied Amidox accumulating as metabolites in the effluent perfusate after liver perfusion.

N-(hydroxyphenyl)glycine can be detected in rat urine as a trimethylsilyled derivative with an ion peak at m/z 311 and a retention time of 14.2 min, which matches the mass spectrum of standard N-(hydroxyphenyl)glycine.
Esterification of p-hydroxy cinnamic acid does not affect its antioxidant activity against AAPH-induced haemolysis.
The 3-hydroxyphenyl group in piperidinone-based fXa inhibitors does not affect factor Xa inhibitory activity, allowing its use as a handle for introducing solubilizing groups.
2,4-dihydroxyphenethylamine inhibits [3H]norepinephrine uptake into murine heart in vivo with an ED50 of 1.69 pmol/kg.
The 8-sulfophenylxanthine derivative PSB-603 is metabolically highly stable in human, rat, and mouse.
N-(hydroxyphenyl)glycine is a metabolite of 3-(phenylamino)alanine in rats, with a common fragmentation pattern in GC/MS analysis as observed in urinary samples.
2-(methylamino)-1-(2-thienyl)propan-1-one is a compound with the molecular formula C8H12NOS, as determined by HR-ESIMS (m/z 170.0632 for [M+H]+).
5-(3,5-dihydroxyphenyl)-4-hydroxyvaleric acid is the major metabolite produced by rat intestinal bacteria.
Quinoxalin-2-mercapto-acetyl-urea analogs display good stability in human liver microsomes and show little or no inhibition of P450 3A4.
m-HPAA, a monophenol metabolite of rutin, has been identified in the blood of animals after oral administration of rutin.
2-(methylamino)-1-(thiophen-2-yl)propan-1-one (thiothinone, bk-MPA) is a bioisosteric counterpart of the psychostimulant methcathinone.
2-(methylamino)-1-(2-thienyl)propan-1-one can be formed by reductive amination of the 2-ketone group, potentially from 1-(2-thienyl)propane-1,2-dione as a minor impurity.
KR-33028 has an oral LD50 value greater than 1500 mg/kg in rats, indicating relatively low toxicity in preclinical studies.
Nitro thiophene-2-carboxamide has been used as a narrow spectrum antibacterial lead compound.
3,4-dihydroxyhydrocinnamate lowers hepatic HMG-CoA reductase activity and hepatic cholesterol in rats fed a high cholesterol diet.

Thiophene-2-carbonyl-guanidine is an NHE-1 inhibitor with an IC50 of 2.0 µM and exhibits potent cardioprotective effects by inhibiting intracellular Ca2+ overload and mitochondrial cell death pathways.

KR-33028 is a cardioprotective agent under preclinical evaluation for ischemia and reperfusion injury, shown to recover cardiac contractability above 60% of basal rate pressure product, attenuate cardiac contracture, and protect myocyte damage in the isolated rat heart ischemia model.

KR-33028 is a bicyclic acylguanidine compound under evaluation as a cardioprotective agent for ischemia and reperfusion injury, with an oral LD50 greater than 1500 mg/kg in rats and a relatively low toxicity profile.
KR-33028 significantly limits infarct size in the in vivo rat myocardial infarction model.
Biflavonols are significant in-vitro inhibitors of CYP enzymes in human liver microsomes.

BA-CoA conjugates are formed during bile acid biosynthesis, and their levels in hiHep lysates are lower than in PHH lysates, potentially due to reduced expression of BA-CoA synthetase and BA-CoA:amino acid N-acetyltransferase.

Allyl[6-[4-(4-bromophenyl)-2H-chromen-7-yloxy]hexyl]methylamine inhibits cholesterol synthesis in primary rat hepatocytes, with its efficacy and potency determined by measuring residual inhibition after 2, 8, or 24 h incubation.
Heptafluorostearate has been demonstrated to be taken up into isolated hepatocytes.
Solanesyl phosphate stimulates N-glycosylation of proteins in cultured AH 70Btc hepatoma cells.
CIS-avicennol, a pyranocoumarin, increases UGT1A1-dependent bilirubin glucuronoconjugation activity by 2-fold in cultured rat and human hepatocytes at 25 µM after 3 days of treatment.
Endobil inhibits bilirubin absorption by rat liver sections via a competitive inhibition mechanism.
Bilirubin ditaurine does not require Mrp2 for efficient elimination in bile.
Bromotamoxifen does not undergo metabolic activation in rat liver.
The conversion of 3H-squalene to sterols by rat liver microsomes and cytosol is inhibited by individual rat and human plasma lipoproteins at various concentrations.

5a-androstane-3a, 17β-diol 17-sulphate undergoes metabolism in isolated hepatocytes involving first-order kinetics (k = 0.021 min⁻¹ x g cells⁻¹) for the disappearance of its monosulphate form, indicating rapid uptake and sulphation of the 3β-hydroxysteroid.
Cis-avicennol treatment for 3 days increases Bili-UGT microsomal activity by 1.5- to 2.5-fold in human hepatocytes from donor 1.
Tetradecylthiopropionic-CoA facilitates translocation of enzymes from cytosol to microsomes in rat liver cell-free extract in vitro.
Androstane disulfate metabolism in adult female rat liver is mediated by cytochrome P-450i, which is responsible for the sex-specific microsomal 15γ-hydroxylase system.
Glucoerysolin is a major contributor to the negative direction of PC-2 in a model for colonic EROD activity regulation.
Co-incubation of hepatocytes with DPPD and nafenopin at 300 µM nafenopin results in a higher specific activity of peroxisomal acyl CoA oxidase than with 300 µM nafenopin alone.
Harmol glucuronidation in rat liver is relatively insensitive to hypoxia, but sensitivity increases markedly in fasted animals.
The conversion of Hydroquinone-glutathione to a di-glutathione conjugate in hepatocytes requires rate constants that are optimized to account for mass balance in metabolic studies.
DPPD does not affect the induction of peroxisomal acyl CoA oxidase activity by nafenopin in rat hepatocytes.
Hydroquinone-glutathione is used as a substrate to study hydroquinone metabolism in hepatocytes, including optimization of substrate concentration and incubation time.
1isopropenylcyclohexanol undergoes catalytic asymmetric oxidation at 50°C with hydrogen peroxide and a Cu/Au catalyst to yield (R)-59 with excellent chemical and optical yields.
Dichloro-1,2-diaminocyclohexane (DACH) forms a platinum(II) complex (DACH:platinum(II)) that exhibits good antitumor activity but is virtually insoluble in water and was not evaluated clinically.

Derivatives of 1-hydroxycyclopentanecarboxylic acid can possess antitussive activity, with some compounds showing potency equal to one half that of codeine in suppressing cough in cats under chemical irritation.

IP-1,2-O is the main metabolic product in rodent liver microsomes and is rapidly transformed into the diol primarily through nonenzymatic hydrolysis with a half-life of 85 minutes in Tris-HCl buffer at pH 7.4 and 37°C.
NAm protects against nitric oxide-induced neuronal injury by blocking and reversing programmed cell death pathways in vitro.

2,4-dichloro-2,6-dihydroxybenzene (2,4-diCl DHB) binding to DHBD results in reduced catalytic activity (k cat app) and Michaelis constant (K mA app) compared to DHB, with k cat app / K mA app being only 4-fold lower for 2,4-diCl DHB.
1,4-dichlorophenol can be degraded by photocatalysis using TiO2, and its degradation is improved when tungsten is incorporated into TiO2 (W5þ-TiO2).
2,3,7,7-tetrachlorodibenzo-p-dioxin (TCDD) exposure decreases glucose uptake by adipose tissue and pancreas and impairs insulin secretion.
Neonatal exposure to MXC in male rodents inhibits organogenesis of accessory reproductive organs and decreases plasma testosterone levels.

IP-1,2-O has been shown to form adducts at the N-terminal valine of hemoglobin in the blood of mice and rats administered IP or a commercially available IP monoepoxide mixture (IPO commercial) containing about 95% IP-1,2-O and 5% IP-3,4-O.
2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxan-4-one-substituted compound 6f, a potent acetylcholinesterase (AChE) inhibitor, has the highest AChE inhibitory activity in biological experiments.

Dichloro (1,2-diaminocyclohexane) Platinum (II) can be encapsulated in PBA-PEG-PLGA micellar nanocarriers designed to specifically target sialic acid (SA) overexpressed on the surface of cancer cells.
Dichloropropylene oxide shows a pronounced decrease in mutagenicity upon the addition of rat liver $9 fractions, which is attributed to membrane-bound hydrolase activity.
3-chloro-3-pyridinol is used as a reagent for esterification with carboxylic acids such as methyl-1H-indole-5-carboxylate to synthesize 3-chloropyridinyl ester-derived SARS-CoV-2 3CLpro inhibitors.
1,4-dioxaspiro[2.3]hexane is a versatile building block used in the synthesis of cell signaling and immunomodulatory molecules such as dihydrosphingosines, sphingosines, and phytosphingosines.
Significant accumulation of CEOOH in LDL (up to nmol/mg LDL protein) is associated with a linear decrease in LDL vitamin E content, with up to a 70% loss in some samples.

CEOOH (cholesterol oxidation oxidation product) accumulates in LDL during and after ultracentrifugation-based isolation methods, with levels as low as pmol/mg Ubiquinol-10 Vitamin E LDL protein after centrifugation.
CEOOH isomers are involved in the initiation of lipid peroxidation, which is a key step leading to the formation of oxidized LDL and foam cells associated with atherosclerosis.

CEOOH isomers are formed in oxidized LDL and their distribution can be evaluated using the DPPP-TLC blot/GC-EI-MS method, which aids in understanding the mechanisms contributing to atherogenic oxidized LDL formation in vivo.
Removal of EDTA by dialysis leads to rapid accumulation of CEOOH in LDL, even when precautions are taken to minimize oxidation.
